name: Texas Red Chili Con Carne
recipeIngredient: 1 tablespoon cumin seeds, crushed into 1 1/2-inch cubes
2 tablespoons masa harina (Mexican corn meal flour)
recipeInstructions:
1. Put the meat through the coarse blade of a meat grinder.
2. Brown in small batches in the bacon fat in a large skillet over moderately high heat.
3. Transfer the cooked meat to a second large heavy skillet using a slotted spoon.
4. Wash the peppers in cold water. Discard the stems and seeds.
5. Tear the peppers into 2-inch pieces.
6. Place the pieces in a small saucepan with the first measure of (cold) water. Cover.
7. Simmer for 20 minutes. Drain, reserving the cooking water.
8. Peel the skin from the peppers. Add to the work bowl of a food processor.
9. Add the reserved water.
10. Puree with short pulses.
11. Mix the pepper puree into the beef.
12. Add the second measure of water. Bring to a boil over high heat.
13. Reduce heat to a slow simmer. Cover.
14. Simmer for 30 minutes.
15. Stir in all the remaining ingredients except the masa harina. Cover.
16. Simmer for 45 minutes.
17. Mix in the masa harina. Cover.
18. Reduce heat to the lowest possible. Cook for 30 minutes longer, stirring occasionally so that the mixture doesn't stick.
19. If too thick, thin with small amounts of boiling water.
20. Serve.
